en: Avetta PQF - Algeria Company Information

**Sole Proprietorship** A sole proprietorship indicates that a business is owned and operated by a single individual.


**General Partnership with Joint Liability (SNC)** An SNC is a partnership formed by at least two individuals or entities who operate a business jointly and severally liable for all partnership debts and obligations.


**Limited Partnership (SCS)** An SCS is a hybrid partnership composed of general partners—who manage the business and bear unlimited liability—and limited partners, who contribute capital but do not participate in management. Their liability is limited to the amount of their contributions.


**Limited Liability Company (SARL)** A SARL is a private company form that separates the legal identity of the business from its owners (partners or shareholders).


**Single-Member Limited Liability Company (EURL)** An EURL is a variant of the SARL, with a single shareholder who enjoys the same limited liability protection as in a standard SARL.


**Joint Stock Company (SPA)** The SPA is the commercial company form typically used for larger enterprises.


**Simplified Joint Stock Company (SAS)** The SAS is a flexible hybrid commercial company introduced to better meet the needs of startups and innovative businesses.


**Foreign Representative Office** Foreign Representative Offices in Algeria serve exclusively as non-commercial liaison entities.

The Trade Register Number is your company's unique registration number assigned to every registered business in Algeria. The Trade Register Extract is the official document issued by the [National Center of the Trade Register (CNRC)](https://sidjilcom.cnrc.dz/en) that proves a company’s legal existence.

A [Tax Identification Number (NIF)](https://www.mfdgi.gov.dz/) is a unique tax number required for all tax compliance, invoice issuance, imports/exports, and dealings with customs and the Directorate of Taxes.
